items.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
items.summary: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

items.summary: As the AI legal services industry heats up, Anthropic is launching its own suite of features designed to assist law firms.

items.title: Android adds a feature to stop you from doomscrolling
items.summary: Google’s new Android feature, Pause Point, forces you to wait before opening distracting apps in an effort to curb addictive scrolling habits.

items.title: Google unveils Googlebooks, a new line of AI-native laptops
items.summary: The company says Googlebooks, which are launching this fall, are the first laptops designed from the ground up for Gemini Intelligence to offer personal and proactive help.

items.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
items.summary: Intrusion Logging is a new part of Android’s Advanced Protection Mode, which aims to help protect human rights activists, journalists, and dissidents from government spyware attack and law enforcement forensic devices.

items.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all

The company also announced some updates for cars that run on Google's built-in native software, including the ability to […]

items.title: Gemini’s biggest new features are all about controlling your phone

items.title: Lectric Memorial Day Sale offers up to $694 in FREE gear + up to $500 price cuts on e-bikes starting from $799
items.summary:
Lectric has just launched its Memorial Day e-bike Sale, one of the brand’s biggest sale events of the year, not to mention of summer, and you’re able to score up to $720 total savings: up to $694 in FREE bundled gear alongside two models getting up to $500 price drops. Many of the brand’s popular e-bikes are getting increased bundle packages during this event, like my personal pick for all-around commuting versatility, the XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike that comes with $473 in FREE gear at $1,299 shipped, while the standard XP4 e-bike is getting a FREE $227 bundle at $999 shipped. Without the savings on the bundles, these packages would normally run you $1,772 and $1,226, respectively. The standard XP4 bundles contain the usual LevelUp rack, suspension seat post, and accordion bike lock, while the XP4 750 e-bike gets those alongside an Elite headlight, a phone mount, a pair of mirrors, and a water-resistant pannier bag. Head below to learn more (or check out our hands-on review here) and browse the full lineup of Memorial Day deals.
